name: browser-use
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); LLM API costs apply (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5); no SDK costs

auth.notes: OpenAI/Anthropic API key for LLM; optional BrowserBase cloud for headless

performance.notes: 3-15s per browser action due to multimodal LLM page reasoning

agent_readiness.notes: Python library for AI-driven web browser automation. Agent class takes natural language task and autonomously navigates web to complete it. Playwright backend. Multimodal LLM (GPT-4o/Claude) sees page state via screenshots and DOM. Memory, history, structured output. Supports multi-step tasks across multiple pages. Most popular Python browser agent library (30K+ GitHub stars). LangChain/LangGraph integration.

gotchas: Non-deterministic — agents can take unexpected paths or fail mid-task
High LLM cost per task (~$0.05-0.50 per complex task with GPT-4o)
Captchas and bot detection can block agents
Still early — reliability varies significantly by website complexity
best_when: Automating web tasks that can't be done via API
Web research agents that need to navigate arbitrary sites
Testing web UIs with natural language scenarios
Building personal automation assistants for web tasks
avoid_when: High-volume or production-scale scraping (cost and reliability)
When stable APIs exist for the target service
Security-sensitive workflows (agent has full browser access)
summary: browser-use is the most popular Python library for AI-driven web browser automation with 30K+ GitHub stars. Playwright backend with multimodal LLM (GPT-4o/Claude) for natural language task execution. Agent navigates web autonomously to complete multi-step tasks. LangChain/LangGraph integration. Non-deterministic and high LLM cost — best for research and automation when APIs don't exist.
